Name: Mechanical Kitchen Timer
Description: ERB rulebook modeling a 5-part mechanical kitchen timer. Every noun in the README is a table or field: case, winding knob (with dial, shaft, scale, pointer), mainspring (in a drum) on an arbor, a three-gear gear train driving an output shaft, an escapement (toothed wheel + two-pronged pallet regulated by a hairspring), and a bell hammer (arm + striker held by a catch released by a cam on the arbor) striking a bell. The sample data tells a narrative: at 07:45 the Chef wound the Egg Timer for a soft-boiled egg; at 07:55 it rang. At 09:30 the Baker wound the Pasta Timer for a 20-minute simmer — still ticking. At 10:00 the Chef wound the Roast Timer to its full 60 minutes. Each timer captures a different snapshot of the same mechanism's lifecycle: rung, mid-run, and just-wound.

schema.name: WindCount
schema.Description: Total wind actions performed by this user.

schema.name: CookCount
schema.Description: Total cooks this user has prepared today.

schema.name: CompletedCookCount
schema.Description: Cooks this user has prepared that have finished (their timer has rung).

schema.name: ActivityLevel
schema.Description: Fuzzy take on how active this user is today: idle if no winds, casual for one or two, busy for three or more.

schema.name: TotalRecipeMinutesAttempted
schema.Description: Sum of recommended-minutes across recipes this user has cooked today. Zig-zags User -> Cook -> Recipe.RecommendedMinutes.

schema.name: AvgRecipeMinutesAttempted
schema.Description: Mean recommended-minutes across this user's cooks today. Zig-zags User -> Cook -> Recipe.RecommendedMinutes.

schema.name: CookingStyle
schema.Description: Fuzzy verdict on this user's day, off the zig-zag aggregate AvgRecipeMinutesAttempted: 'snacking' under 10 min average, 'short-order' under 30, 'slow-cooking' beyond.

Timers.Description: Top-level timer assembly. Holds the raw mechanical parameters and the headline runtime DAG: stored torque, remaining minutes, wind state, and whether the bell has rung.

schema.name: WindAngleDegrees
schema.Description: Current angular position of the winding knob, 0..MaxWindDegrees.

schema.name: MaxWindDegrees
schema.Description: Full-wind knob travel; 360 deg = 60 min on a standard dial.

schema.name: MaxSpringTorqueNm
schema.Description: Mainspring torque at full wind, newton-meters.

schema.name: WindFraction
schema.Description: Fraction of full wind currently stored.

schema.name: StoredTorqueNm
schema.Description: Torque currently stored in the mainspring (§2).

schema.name: RemainingMinutes
schema.Description: Minutes left until the cam trips §5.

schema.name: WindState
schema.Description: Coarse state: Unwound, Partial, or Full.

schema.name: IsArmed
schema.Description: True while the mainspring still holds energy.

schema.name: TickCount
schema.Description: Total escapement ticks recorded for this timer.

schema.name: RingCount
schema.Description: Total ding events recorded for this timer.

schema.name: CookCount
schema.Description: Number of cooks currently or recently timed on this timer.

schema.name: TotalRecipeMinutesUsed
schema.Description: Sum of recommended minutes across recipes the cooks on this timer are following. Zig-zags through Cook to Recipe.

schema.name: AvgRecipeMinutesUsed
schema.Description: Average recommended-minutes across the recipes any cook on this timer is following. Goes Timer -> Cook (rollup) -> Recipe (the underlying lookup).

schema.name: WindDurationMinutes
schema.Description: How many minutes one full wind buys on this timer. Derived from MaxWindDegrees at 6 deg per minute.

schema.name: ExpectedTicksPerMinute
schema.Description: Escapement ticks expected per minute on this timer.

schema.name: ExpectedTotalTicks
schema.Description: Ticks expected across a full wind, end to end.

schema.name: TicksElapsedExpected
schema.Description: Ticks that should have occurred so far on this timer based on how much the spring has unwound.

schema.name: TickEfficiencyPercent
schema.Description: Observed ticks divided by expected ticks so far, as a percentage. A timer that has just been wound returns 100 since no ticks were expected yet.

schema.name: HasSparseTickRecord
schema.Description: True when the recorded tick count is well below what should have happened by now.

schema.name: MechanicalPhase
schema.Description: Fuzzy lifecycle phase. A timer is freshly-wound if barely any of its wind has been spent, mid-run if it is well into its travel, nearly-done if it is almost out of energy, and rung once it has fired.

Cases.Description: The outer housing of a timer. The bell is mounted on top of the case.

Bells.Description: The bell mounted on top of the case. Struck by the hammer striker when §5 fires.

schema.name: TimesRung
schema.Description: Number of ding events for this bell.

schema.name: HasEverRung
schema.Description: True if at least one ding has been recorded.

WindingKnobs.Description: §1 — the dial on the front. Shaft is fixed to the mainspring arbor. Rim carries a printed scale 0..60 read against a fixed pointer on the case.

schema.name: ScaleMin
schema.Description: Lower bound of the printed scale.

schema.name: ScaleMax
schema.Description: Upper bound of the printed scale.

schema.name: PointerFixed
schema.Description: True if the case pointer is fixed (it always is on this design).

schema.name: ScaleReading
schema.Description: Number the printed scale currently shows against the pointer.

Arbors.Description: The shaft driven by the mainspring. Connects §1 (knob shaft) to §2 (mainspring), and carries the §5 cam.

Mainsprings.Description: §2 — flat coiled steel spring in a drum. Inner end anchors to the arbor; outer end anchors to the case.

schema.name: InnerEndAnchor
schema.Description: Where the inner end anchors (always Arbor).

schema.name: OuterEndAnchor
schema.Description: Where the outer end anchors (always Case).

GearTrains.Description: §3 — the three-gear stack between mainspring arbor and escapement. Carries the output shaft that drives the dial pointer.

schema.name: ReductionRatio
schema.Description: Total speed-up from arbor to escapement.

schema.name: OutputShaftRPM
schema.Description: Speed of the output shaft (the one carrying the dial pointer).

schema.name: GearCount
schema.Description: Number of gears in this train. Always 3 on this design.

schema.name: TotalTeeth
schema.Description: Sum of tooth counts across the three gears.

schema.name: AverageToothCount
schema.Description: Mean tooth count across the gears in this train.

Gears.Description: Individual gears in a gear train. Each has a tooth count and a position in the chain (1, 2, or 3).

schema.name: PositionInChain
schema.Description: 1, 2, or 3 — arbor side to escapement side.

schema.name: ToothCount
schema.Description: Number of teeth on this gear.

schema.name: IsFirstInChain
schema.Description: True for the gear nearest the arbor.

schema.name: IsLastInChain
schema.Description: True for the gear nearest the escapement.

schema.name: SizeClass
schema.Description: Fuzzy size based on tooth count: large 40+, medium 25-39, small under 25.

schema.name: OnTimer
schema.Description: Timer the gear's gear-train belongs to (chained lookup through GearTrains).

OutputShafts.Description: The shaft at the end of the gear train. Carries the dial pointer.

schema.name: CurrentRPM
schema.Description: Inherits the gear train's output RPM.

DialPointers.Description: Pointer mounted on the output shaft. Reads against the printed scale on the knob rim.

schema.name: OnOutputShaft
schema.Description: Shaft this pointer is mounted on.

Escapements.Description: §4 — escapement assembly: toothed wheel plus two-pronged pallet plus hairspring. Defines the time base.

schema.name: HasTicked
schema.Description: True if this escapement has recorded at least one tick.

EscapementWheels.Description: The toothed wheel of the escapement. Each pallet rock releases exactly one tooth.

schema.name: ToothCount
schema.Description: Number of teeth around the wheel.

Pallets.Description: Pivoting piece sitting across the escapement-wheel teeth. Two prongs (the README's 'two-pronged pallet'). Rocking is set by pallet mass plus the hairspring.

schema.name: ProngCount
schema.Description: Number of prongs. Always 2 on this design.

Hairsprings.Description: Tiny coiled spring attached to the pallet. Sets the pallet's rocking rate together with pallet mass.

Cams.Description: Profile on the arbor of §2. Its notch aligns with the hammer catch at the zero position; that alignment is what trips §5.

schema.name: NotchAngleDeg
schema.Description: Arbor angle at which the notch aligns with the catch.

BellHammers.Description: §5 — spring-loaded arm with a steel striker, held back by the hammer catch. When the cam notch reaches the catch, the catch releases and the arm's own spring snaps the striker against the bell.

schema.name: ArmTorqueNm
schema.Description: Spring torque stored in the hammer arm.

schema.name: TimerHasRung
schema.Description: Whether the parent timer has rung.

schema.name: ReleaseState
schema.Description: Held while the cam still restrains the catch; Released once the timer has rung.

HammerArms.Description: The spring-loaded arm of the bell hammer. Carries the striker at its tip.

Strikers.Description: The small steel head at the tip of the hammer arm. Whatever actually contacts the bell.

schema.name: OnArm
schema.Description: Hammer arm this striker is mounted on.

HammerCatches.Description: Catch that holds the hammer back until the cam notch aligns. Once released, the arm spring snaps the striker against the bell.

schema.name: IsEngaged
schema.Description: True while the catch still restrains the hammer.

WindActions.Description: Event: a user rotates the winding knob to a target angle at a point in time. The README's §1 input event.

schema.name: AppliedToTimer
schema.Description: Timer the turned knob is fixed to.

schema.name: ResultedInRing
schema.Description: True if the timer this wind action wound has since rung.

TickEvents.Description: Event: the escapement releases one tooth — one 'tick' from the README.

schema.name: OnTimerThatHasRung
schema.Description: Whether the parent timer has rung.

RingEvents.Description: Event: the bell hammer strikes the bell — the README's 'ding'. Recorded once per fired §5.

Cooks.Description: A dish being timed on a timer. Each cook started at the moment a user wound the timer; its status mirrors the timer's lifecycle.

schema.name: StartedAt
schema.Description: When the dish was started — i.e. when the timer was wound.

schema.name: TimerHasRung
schema.Description: Whether the cook's timer has rung yet.

schema.name: TimerIsArmed
schema.Description: Whether the cook's timer is still armed.

schema.name: TimerRemainingMin
schema.Description: Minutes the cook's timer has left.

schema.name: Status
schema.Description: Done if the timer has rung, Cooking if it is still armed, otherwise Pending.

schema.name: TimerWindDuration
schema.Description: Maximum minutes the chosen timer can run.

schema.name: TimerCanCoverRecipe
schema.Description: True if the chosen timer can run for at least as long as the recipe calls for.

schema.name: SuitabilityVerdict
schema.Description: Fuzzy verdict on the cook's choice of timer: insufficient if the timer is too short for the recipe, overkill if it can run more than three times as long as needed, otherwise appropriate.

schema.name: PreparedByName
schema.Description: Display name of the user preparing this cook.

schema.name: PreparedByActivityLevel
schema.Description: Activity-level verdict on the user preparing this cook (chained lookup through Users).

schema.name: RecipeDurationCategory
schema.Description: Duration-category of the recipe being followed (chained lookup through Recipes).

schema.name: RecipeTempProfile
schema.Description: Cooking-temp profile of the recipe (chained lookup through Recipes).

schema.name: TimerMechanicalPhase
schema.Description: Mechanical phase of the timer this cook is on (chained lookup through Timers).

Recipes.Description: Cooking recipes a cook can follow. Each names a dish and the minutes it should run.

schema.name: IdealTemperatureC
schema.Description: Recommended cooking temperature, in degrees Celsius.

schema.name: DurationCategory
schema.Description: A loose verbal classification of how long this recipe takes: quick under 5 min, short under 15, medium under 45, long beyond that.

schema.name: CookingTempProfile
schema.Description: Loose verbal classification of cooking temperature: boiling, hot oven, warm oven, or low.

schema.name: AvgTimerCapacity
schema.Description: Average wind-duration of the timers cooks have used for this recipe. Zig-zags Recipe -> Cook -> Timer.WindDurationMinutes.

schema.name: TotalTimerCapacity
schema.Description: Sum of timer wind-durations allocated to this recipe today. Zig-zags Recipe -> Cook -> Timer.WindDurationMinutes.

schema.name: AssignmentVerdict
schema.Description: Fuzzy verdict on how well this recipe's actual timer assignments match what it needs. Drives off the zig-zag aggregate AvgTimerCapacity vs the recipe's own RecommendedMinutes.

Kitchens.Description: A kitchen, top of the rollup tree. Aggregates everything below it.

schema.name: TotalTimers
schema.Description: Count of timers in the world.

schema.name: RecipesUnderProvisioned
schema.Description: Recipes whose chosen timer can't cover them.

schema.name: RecipesWellMatched
schema.Description: Recipes whose timer assignment was well-matched.

schema.name: TotalRecipeMinutes
schema.Description: Sum of recommended-minutes across all recipes.

schema.name: HasAnyTimerRung
schema.Description: True if any timer has rung today.

schema.name: HasMisprovisionedRecipe
schema.Description: True if any recipe is currently under-provisioned.

schema.name: OperationalStatus
schema.Description: Fuzzy overall status: 'quiet' if no cooks active, 'busy' if many, 'active' otherwise.

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Hand-authored ERB rulebook for the kitchen-timer README. 18 tables span every noun. Headline DAG: WindAngleDegrees -> WindFraction -> StoredTorqueNm; WindAngleDegrees -> RemainingMinutes -> WindState -> IsArmed -> HasRung; EscapementBaseHz -> GearTrain.OutputShaftRPM -> OutputShaft.CurrentRPM; Timer.HasRung -> BellHammer.ReleaseState -> HammerCatch.IsEngaged; RingEvents -> Bells.TimesRung -> Bells.HasEverRung. Sample data is a single morning's narrative: Chef winds Egg Timer at 07:45, it rings at 07:55; Baker winds Pasta Timer at 09:30 (still ticking); Chef winds Roast Timer at 10:00 (just started). The three timers exercise every IF branch — Unwound/Partial/Full, Armed/Rung, HasEverRung true/false.
_meta._postgres_export: Postgres-computed. Raw fields stored as-is; calculated/lookup/aggregation fields are witnessed inferences.
